speaker: interviewer
text: This is tape number five. Gienia Weinstein. December 21, 1995. Gienia, what is this that we're looking at here?

speaker: interviewee
text: And we left Brunnlitz to Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: What does it say? That you work there?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, this doesn't say. I work on-- this was the fact that they give us in Czechoslovakia language, and they give me for it-- this was the name from my first husband. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is after the war, when I got married.

speaker: interviewee
text: With my husband. This is the sign when you got married.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] This is a-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] when I was born, they took-- they-- you know how you call-- where I was born.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, the name from my first husband. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Yeah, name with my first husband.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Tell us, who is this? Who's the picture on the left?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is, on the left, my father.

speaker: interviewee
text: Before the war. This is before the war, you know, in Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know about what year? [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, and on the right, who is that?

speaker: interviewer
text: And this was taken before the war?

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was she wearing that you said religious people wore? You said-- what was she wearing? Tell us.

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, she was wearing a sheitel.

speaker: interviewee
text: A wig, sheitel. A wig. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Gienia, who is this? Who's the woman on the left?

speaker: interviewer
text: And the last name, do you know?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the right is my sister, Tobeleh Raizel.

speaker: interviewee
text: Oh, long time before the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: How did you get these pictures?

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know. I got. I don't know how I got the pictures. Somebody maybe give me, but I didn't got anything back. And everything kind of-- my sister's kids, I don't have no pictures.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. My parents, my sister, and their three small kids in the woods.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] OK, Gienia, let's start from the left.

speaker: interviewee
text: The left is my first husband, Leon Gams. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Then is my younger brother, Moishe Nornberg. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] And then some friends.

speaker: interviewer
text: You don't know who the others are?

speaker: interviewee
text: Ah, this is a long, long time. This can be before the war. This is old.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this taken? [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my oldest brother, Zelik Nornberg.

speaker: interviewer
text: Also Sigmund, you call him? You also call him Sigmund?

speaker: interviewee
text: This, I think after the war. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And-- and they-- they tell you when I-- I can take this. I think this is after-- yeah. This is after the war. I think this was-- I don't know where. Or in Israel? I think in Israel.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my youngest brother. This is in Israel, '48.

speaker: interviewee
text: Helena. Hudess. This is in the-- Helena they call her here. Helena Feldsott. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Ah, this was about two years ago.

speaker: interviewee
text: Oh, we've got to answer questions?

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Gienia, why don't you introduce us to your family? Who's here?

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, OK, this is my granddaughter on the right.

speaker: interviewee
text: Beth Weinstein. [LAUGHING] On the left is Justin Weinstein.

speaker: interviewee
text: And behind is my son and my daughter-in-law.

speaker: interviewee
text: My son is-- I call him [NON-ENGLISH] Weinstein. And my daughter-in-law is Laurie Weinstein.

speaker: interviewer
text: What do other people call your son, besides [NON-ENGLISH]? What's his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's a Polish name, Ted. And we always call him [NON-ENGLISH].

speaker: interviewer
text: Is there something that you'd like to say on this tape, Ted, or Laurie?

speaker: interviewee
text: Laurie, you wanted to say something?

speaker: interviewer
text: How you feel about your mother or mother-in-law giving her testimony?

speaker: interviewee
text: think it's great because it's good to have, you know, records of people who were actually involved and to keep it known to people so that what happened before won't happen again. And to really--

speaker: interviewee
text: Definitely. And to prove the people who say, oh, it never happened, to prove them wrong. Because how could so many people have stories? They would never want to make it up. It would be really a sick mind to do that.

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, the only that I'd like to say is that we are very fortunate that my mother survived. And I try to explain to the kids how lucky they are. Because if Bubbe wouldn't survive, they probably wouldn't be here today. And that's what I try to-- that's what I try to teach them. It's hard for them to understand at this age.

speaker: interviewee
text: It really was, but I think, as they get older, they will.

speaker: interviewer
text: Justin, did you have anything you wanted to add? [LAUGHING] And you, Beth, was there something you wanted to say about your Bubbe or--

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know. Actually, my mom and my dad actually kind of said it-- that-- that I'm kind of lucky to be here. Because if she wasn't-- if she--

speaker: interviewee
text: If like she wasn't around, then I wouldn't be here. So I should be lucky. I am growing up, so I'd like to learn more about it. Basically--

speaker: interviewee
text: Jus, what about at school? We talked about it today.

speaker: interviewee
text: Mom talked about it at my school today. Because she was going to come in and give us latkes and talk about the Hanukkah, and then she told them about it also.

speaker: interviewer
text: What did she tell them about what?

speaker: interviewee
text: Like about that-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I think that, for people-- because she said I was going to be on this tape, and then-- and then she said this is to, like, remind people about the war so that they all know not to like it like that.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Thank you so much. Thank you for coming, and thank you for sharing.
